SunburstChart.heroDescription: Free online sunburst chart maker. Visualize hierarchical part-to-whole data with concentric rings, customize labels and colors, and preview the structure instantly.
SunburstChart.whyItWorksDescription: A sunburst chart helps people read both hierarchy and share at the same time, moving from the center outward without switching between multiple charts or tables.
SunburstChart.comparisonPoint1: See how top-level categories split into smaller groups while still preserving the total structure.
SunburstChart.comparisonPoint2: Follow parent-child paths from the center to the outer rings when you need to explain layered composition.
SunburstChart.comparisonPoint3: Compare structure and contribution in one compact view when a regular pie chart is too shallow.
SunburstChart.previewDescription1: A sunburst chart sits between a pie chart and a tree map: it keeps the circular part-to-whole feeling while exposing the path from parent to child.
SunburstChart.previewDescription2: Use it when your story is not only which category is largest, but also how that category splits into smaller pieces over multiple levels.
SunburstChart.productHighlightsTitle: Built for fast hierarchy input, live preview, and lightweight sunburst customization.
SunburstChart.feature1Title: Enter hierarchy rows in a table
SunburstChart.feature1Description: Add one row per branch with up to three levels, then update names and values directly inside the editor.
SunburstChart.feature2Description: As soon as you change a value or rename a node, the chart rebalances the hierarchy in the preview area.
SunburstChart.feature3Title: Switch between colorful and single-tone palettes
SunburstChart.feature3Description: Use distinct colors for top-level groups or keep the whole chart aligned to one theme color.
SunburstChart.feature4Description: Keep labels visible for explanation-heavy charts or hide them when you want a cleaner export.
SunburstChart.useCasesTitle: Use it for revenue mix, category trees, budget breakdowns, and multi-level composition reports.
SunburstChart.useCase1: Break revenue into region, channel, and product group within one hierarchy.
SunburstChart.useCase2: Show website traffic from source to campaign to landing page.
SunburstChart.useCase3: Explain how a total budget flows from department to team to project.
SunburstChart.useCase4: Summarize product catalogs from category to subcategory to SKU family.
SunburstChart.useCase5: Present organizational structures where each ring adds a new level of detail.
SunburstChart.useCase6: Compare multi-level portfolio or inventory composition without using multiple nested charts.
SunburstChart.decisionGuide1Point1: Your data has a clear parent-child hierarchy with two to four levels.
SunburstChart.decisionGuide1Point2: You want to show both part-to-whole share and the path from parent to child.
SunburstChart.decisionGuide1Point3: You need a compact alternative to multiple pie charts or separate grouped tables.
SunburstChart.decisionGuide1Point4: Readers benefit from understanding how large categories split into smaller segments.
SunburstChart.decisionGuide2Point1: You only have one level of categories and a pie chart or bar chart is simpler.
SunburstChart.decisionGuide2Point2: You need precise comparison between similar values and a bar chart is easier to read.
SunburstChart.decisionGuide2Point3: Your labels are very long or the hierarchy is too deep for comfortable ring labeling.
SunburstChart.decisionGuide2Point4: You care more about space efficiency than path readability and a tree map fits better.
SunburstChart.faqTitle: Everything people usually ask before building a sunburst chart.
SunburstChart.faq1Question: What is a sunburst chart best for?
SunburstChart.faq1Answer: A sunburst chart is best for showing hierarchical part-to-whole data when you need to explain both the overall share and how each category breaks down into subcategories.
SunburstChart.faq2Question: How is a sunburst chart different from a pie chart?
SunburstChart.faq2Answer: A pie chart usually shows only one level of categories, while a sunburst chart adds multiple rings so you can display parent-child relationships in the same figure.
SunburstChart.faq3Question: How many levels should a sunburst chart have?
SunburstChart.faq3Answer: Most sunburst charts work best with two to four levels. Beyond that, the outer rings can become crowded and harder to label clearly.
SunburstChart.faq4Question: When should I use a tree map instead?
SunburstChart.faq4Answer: Use a tree map when you want better space efficiency and area comparison. Use a sunburst chart when the parent-child path matters more than rectangle area.
SunburstChart.faq5Question: Can I leave some levels empty?
SunburstChart.faq5Answer: Yes. You can keep later levels empty when a branch stops earlier than others, and the chart will still group the data correctly.
SunburstChart.faq6Question: Why use this tool instead of building the chart manually?
SunburstChart.faq6Answer: This tool focuses on fast table-based input, instant preview, and lightweight customization so you can create a clear sunburst chart without a heavier spreadsheet workflow.
SunburstChart.ctaTitle: Build a sunburst chart in minutes.
SunburstChart.ctaDescription: Enter your hierarchy rows, adjust the visual settings, and use the live preview above to turn layered data into a clear concentric chart.
SunburstChart.editorChartTitlePlaceholder: e.g., Revenue Breakdown by Region and Channel
SunburstChart.editorSingleColorDescription: All top-level groups use shades derived from the theme color.
SunburstChart.editorColorfulDescription: Top-level groups use distinct colors and children inherit lighter shades.
SunburstChart.editorEmptyState: Add at least one valid hierarchy row with a positive value to render the sunburst chart.
